actuated synthetic gecko adhesive is demonstrated. Results show sphere pull-off forces can be increased 10-fold by changing the ridge orientation via the external magnetic field,
orientation via the external magnetic field, and that the effective elastic modulus can be changed from 65 kPa to 1.5 MPa. movie of controllable adhesive Gillies et al. Advanced Functional Materials
esive Gillies et al. Advanced Functional Materials, 2013 Coordinated Launching of an Ornithopter with a Hexapedal Robot (May 2015) We develop a cooperative launching system for a 13.2 gram
e launching system for a 13.2 gram ornithopter micro-aerial vehicle (MAV), the H2Bird, by carrying it on the VelociRoACH. We determine the necessary initial velocity and pitch
ine the necessary initial velocity and pitch angle for take off using force data collected in a wind tunnel and use the VelociRoACH to reach these initial conditions for successful
initial conditions for successful launch. Rose et al. (IEEE ICRA May 2015 ) video Running beyond the bio-inspired regime (May 2015) The X2-VelociRoACH
regime (May 2015) The X2-VelociRoACH is a 54 gram experimental legged robot which was developed to test hypotheses about running with unnaturally high stride frequencies. It is cap
unnaturally high stride frequencies. It is capable of running at stride frequencies up to 45 Hz, and velocities up to 4.9 m/s, making it the fastest legged robot relative to siz
legged robot relative to size. Haldane and Fearing ( IEEE ICRA May 2015) video Anisotropic Leg Spines for Increased Traction (May 2015) Collapsible leg spines found on insects and

2015) Collapsible leg spines found on insects and spiders provide a passive mechanism for increased traction while running over complex terrain. Spiny feet for VelociRoACH reduced dimensionless Cost of Pulling by an order of magnitude while robot spe

f Pulling by an order of magnitude while robot speed while pulling load increased by 50%. Lee and Fearing ( IEEE ICRA May 2015 ) video Detection of Slippery Terrain with Picket Robot (June 2014) Experiments

ery terrain with 92% accuracy. Haldane et al. ( IEEE ICRA June 2014 ) video Roll oscillation modulated turning (June 2014) A new dynamic turning mode in legged robots is demonstrate
turning mode in legged robots is demonstrated, which uses a phase-locked gait to excite coupled height and roll oscillations. A modified robot with enhanced roll oscillations
with enhanced roll oscillations turned at 206 deg/sec at a speed of 0.4 m/s. Haldane and Fearing ( IEEE ICRA June 2014 ) video Aerodynamic Steering
ne 2014 ) video Aerodynamic Steering SailRoACH (Oct. 2013) SailRoACH uses aerodynamic forces on its tail to turn, achieving at 1.2 meter radius turn while running at 1.6
meter radius turn while running at 1.6 meters per second. Scaling laws work favorably for this turning mode for fast running small robots. Kohut et al. (IEEE IROS Nov. 2013) movie A

normal adhesion to prevent falling. Dynamic climbing on a 30 degree slope succeeds at 12 Hz leg rate, but only 4 Hz on a 70 degree slope.

Autonomous 2.4 gram hexapod robot (Sep. 2008) A new 2.4 gram crawling robot was created which uses laser machined glass fiber t
er machined glass fiber to create 57 flexure joints. The robot has on board power and electronics, and a top speed of 3 cm/sec (~ 1 body length per
c (~ 1 body length per second). Hoover IROS 2008 Movie of RoACH Self-Cleaning Gecko Adhesive (Sep. 2008) First synthetic
Gecko Adhesive (Sep. 2008) First synthetic gecko adhesive which cleans itself during use, as the natural gecko does. After contamination by microsp
After contamination by microspheres, the microfiber array loses all adhesion strength. After repeated contacts with clean glass, the
clean glass, the microspheres are shed, and the fibers recover 30% of their original adhesion. The fibers have a non-adhesive default state
on-adhesive default state, which encourages particle removal during contact. Contact Self-Cleaning of Synthetic Gecko Adhesive, Langmuir
Gecko Adhesive, Langmuir 2008 Fast Prototyping for Folded Millirobots (Apr. 2008) By using posterboard, laser cutting, and lamination
oard, laser cutting, and lamination processes, multi-jointed robots can be rapidly prototyped in less than 2 hours. An example 2X scale crawler model has 57 flexure
example 2X scale crawler model has 57 flexure joints and can be driven using shape memory alloy actuation. Hoover ICRA 2008 Fab Process

synthetic gecko adhesive using hard polymer microfibers. Microfiber array using 42 million polypropylene microfibers per square centimeter. Patches
centimeter. Patches can support 9 N/sq.cm. in estimated contact region with preload of just 0.1N/sq.cm. Sliding-induced adhesion
